reasoning_model_1: The model produced the text content accurately with no errors. Regarding fluency, the speech is slow and whispery, fitting a storytelling vibe, but somewhat monotonous. In terms of the core requirement (Dynamic Adjustment of Pitch from Extremely Low to Medium), the model successfully established the starting condition. At 00:00-00:03 ('夜深了...'), the voice is indeed extremely low and deep, utilizing a vocal fry/bass register. However, for the remainder of the clip (00:03-00:09), the voice maintains this extremely low pitch without any noticeable transition to a medium pitch. While it adhered to the style of the starting instruction, it failed to perform the dynamic adjustment requested.
reasoning_model_2: The model produced the text content accurately and the speech is fluent and natural. However, regarding the core requirement, the model failed completely. It did not start with an 'extremely low' pitch; the voice at 00:00 ('夜深了') is a standard medium-pitch male voice. throughout the clip (00:00-00:08), the pitch remains consistent in the medium range with normal prosodic intonation but no structural shift in pitch register. It effectively ignored the specific paralinguistic instructions for both the starting state and the transition.
model_comparison: Both models failed to execute the dynamic transition from low to medium pitch; both audio clips remained relatively static in their respective registers. However, Model 1 is the winner because it successfully adhered to the 'extremely low pitch' instruction for the start of the sentence, creating the requested paralinguistic effect even if it didn't transition out of it. Model 2, in contrast, produced a standard reading that disregarded the 'extremely low' constraint entirely, showing no attempt at paralinguistic control.
